changes_summary: Revised Step 2 to explicitly address FLAG-001: the `buildConfigFromDragResult` function now merges `metaUpdates` (containing overlap-resolution `from`/`to` patches from `resolveGroupOverlaps`) into meta before building the output config. Added detailed explanation in Overview of why `type: 'config'` is needed (materializeData hardcodes `current.tracks`) and why we still run `applyMultiDragMoves` internally (overlap resolution). Added a specific test case in Step 5 that validates the meta-merge behavior. Clarified Step 4 to note that ghost behavior during drag is acceptable as-is (falls back to source position) with an optional polish enhancement.

success_criteria.criterion: Multi-clip drag to the new-track zone (above top row / below bottom row) creates a new track and moves all selected clips with correct relative offsets
Single-clip drag to new track continues to work identically (code path at useClipDrag.ts:364-376 untouched)
Multi-clip drag to existing tracks continues to work identically (the !isNewTrack branch is unchanged)
planMultiDragMoves returns canMove: false when any secondary clip would land out of bounds after the one new track is added
Track kind compatibility is enforced — cannot create a visual track and place audio clips on it

buildConfigFromDragResult merges metaUpdates (including from/to patches from resolveGroupOverlaps) into meta before building the config, so timed clips trimmed by overlap resolution serialize with correct from/to values
New unit tests cover: buildAugmentedData top/bottom, buildConfigFromDragResult with metaUpdates, planMultiDragMoves on augmented data, out-of-bounds rejection, kind mismatch rejection
Drop indicator shows new-track affordance during multi-clip drag near edges
Secondary ghost indicators render correctly when dragging multi-clip selection near timeline edges
New functions are pure (no side effects) and follow existing patterns in multi-drag-utils.ts
No new files created beyond multi-drag-utils.test.ts

assumptions: Rejecting multi-drag when any secondary clip would land out of bounds (even after adding the one new track) is correct — we create exactly one new track, not multiple.
The anchor clip's source track kind determines the new track's kind. All clips in the selection must be the same kind (visual or audio) for the move to succeed.
The type: 'config' edit path is required because materializeData in the type: 'rows' path uses current.tracks which won't include the new track.
Ghost rendering during drag showing relative positions (falling back to sourceRowId) is acceptable. The optional virtualRowIds enhancement in Step 4 is polish.

The existing DropIndicator new-track CSS affordance (drop-target-new-track class) works for multi-drag without changes — computeDropPosition already sets isNewTrack correctly regardless of selection size.
